sanskrit: त्रै-विद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते। ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोकमश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान्॥
transliteration: trai-vidyā māṃ soma-pāḥ pūta-pāpā yajñair iṣṭvā svar-gatiṃ prārthayante | te puṇyam āsādya surendra-lokam aśnanti divyān divi deva-bhogān ||
literal_meaning: The knowers of the three Vedas, soma-drinkers, sins cleansed, having sacrificed, pray for heaven; they, attaining merit, reach Indra’s world and taste divine celestial pleasures.
contextual_meaning: Ritual expertise and moral purity can secure sublime hedonic rewards, but these remain within the cyclic economy of desire and achievement; they do not yet touch the trans-egoic freedom Krishna offers.
psychological_state: Secure-achiever mindset: confident in procedural righteousness yet still bargaining for rewards.
trigger_scenario: A top student follows every rule to get into an elite college, succeeds, but senses the admission letter isn’t the self-worth she hoped for.
anchor_text: Ritual purity buys heavenly pleasure, not the freedom beyond desire.
